sources.note: Base v2.8.2. Re-based from earlier v2.2.0 import onto current upstream: adds Detection Guidance, hard em/en-dash cut, and patterns for diff-anchored writing, manufactured punchlines, aphorism formulas, conversational openers, hyphenated-pair overuse, subjectless fragments, and speculative gap-filling.

modifications: Claude-specific graft (§34-37): post-action summaries, unsolicited ethical/safety caveats, standalone caveat lines, and broad markdown overuse. Originally derived from upstream PR #51 (closed, never merged) and PR #39 (closed).
Source-aware curly-quote rule (§19): skip on Claude output; boldface/inline-header lists flagged as high-confidence Claude tells (§15-16).
Fabrication guardrails on vague attributions (§5): [NEEDS REAL SOURCE] placeholder, never invent citations or named people.
Extended Collaborative Communication Artifacts (§20) with Claude openers ('I'd be happy to', 'Let me [verb]', 'I'll now proceed to').
Added DO NOT TOUCH section (quotes, code/specs, legal wording, 3x-consistent stylistic choices).
Removed upstream Voice Calibration (optional writing-sample voice matching) — unused by the maintainer.
§14 scope clause: the hard em/en-dash cut applies to text being rewritten, not clean human prose left untouched.
